negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Missing chain-halt poison recovery path that reads threshold, detects temporal divergence, evicts scheduled pool transactions, and evicts each call_id from SynchronousScheduler state.
related_but_insufficient_code: maintain.rs explicitly says raw scheduled txpool admission is dormant and the old chain-halt scheduled-tx eviction loop is not resurrected; current tick only removes queued txs and async lifecycle entries.

notes: Confirmed gap; parent-state obligation synthesis is not equivalent to clearing local scheduled pool state.
rationale: The current poison-recovery behavior is the later chain-halt path, not routine age expiry. The effective invariant combines the eviction API, chain-halt trigger, seconds-based timestamp comparison, and all-scheduled eviction path.
statement: At current tip, transaction-pool maintenance must compare wall-clock seconds to the latest block timestamp converted to seconds, and when the divergence exceeds CHAIN_HALT_THRESHOLD_SECS it must evict every scheduled transaction from the scheduled pool and evict each associated call_id from SynchronousScheduler state.
suggested_falsification_tests: Populate the scheduled pool with multiple scheduled transactions, mock the latest header timestamp in milliseconds so seconds-based divergence exceeds CHAIN_HALT_THRESHOLD_SECS, tick maintenance, and assert each scheduled transaction is absent from the pool and each call_id is absent from SynchronousScheduler state.
Repeat with the same millisecond timestamp but no conversion to seconds; the test should fail because poison eviction would not trigger at the intended threshold.
summary: Chain-halt poison eviction clearing scheduled pool/Scheduler calls is absent.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Populate due scheduled calls, make latest header stale beyond threshold, tick txpool maintenance, and assert scheduled pool entries/call IDs are evicted; current port lacks the path.
title: Chain-halt poison eviction clears scheduled pool and Scheduler calls
